Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a falling head permeability test, the following values are given:
Length of soil specimen = 1.5 m
Area of soil specimen = 0.5 m2
Area of stand pipe = 0.01 m2
If head at time, t = 0 is 20 cm and at t = 4 min is 12 cm, then the value of hydraulic conductivity of the soil is _____×10–5 m/sec. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 6.39
Solution

6.39 (6.20 - 6.6)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A soil sample has a compression index of 0.5. The void ratio at a stress of 1.75 kg/cm2 is 0.65. The stress is increased to 3.5 kg/cm2. The value of void ratio and settlement of a soil stratum 6 m thick are respectively:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A clay stratum has 3 m thickness and has an initial overburdern pressure of 40 kN/m2. The clay is over-consolidated with a pre-consolidation pressure of 60 kN/m2. If increase in pressure is 50 kN/m2, then the final settlement due to increase in pressure at the middle of the clay layer is: (Take: Recompression index = 0.05, Compression index = 0.28, Initial void ratio = 1.3)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A soil specimen has a water content of 15% and a wet unit weight of 21 kN/m3. If the specific gravity of soil solids is 2.65, then the void ratio of soil specimen is ________ (Correct upto two decimal places). Take, unit weight of water, = 9.81 kN/m3.

NAT
Correct answer: 0.42
Solution

0.42 (0.40 - 0.45)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A sample of sand of 6 cm height and 5 cm2 cross-sectional area was subjected to piping failure.
The void ratio and specific gravity of soil solids are 0.65 and 2.7 respectively. The seepage force required for piping failure is ______ N. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.30
Solution

0.30 (0.28 - 0.32)

Correct answer: C

The mass of an empty jar was 0.485 kg. When completely filled with water, its mass was 1.650 kg.
Oven dried sample of soil of mass 0.215 kg was placed in the jar and water was added to completely fill the jar and its mass was recorded to be 1.755 kg. The specific gravity of soil solids is_____.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

A soil has a porosity of 35% and water content of 15%. Calculate the weight of water required to be added to 120 cm3 of this soil for full saturation _________ (in gm, correct upto two decimal places). Assume specific gravity of soil solids as 2.7.

NAT
Correct answer: 10.45
Solution

10.45 (10 - 11)

Q14 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Following data are obtained in a shrinkage limit test:
Volume of saturated soil = 15 cm3
Water content of saturated soil = 70%
Soil is dried upto volume of 7 cm3.
Weight of dried soil = 12.5 gm
The value of shrinkage limit of soil is _______ (in %, correct upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 6.01
Solution

6.01 (5.8 - 6.3)

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Correct answer: D
